summary: Pass 13 of the implicit-function-declaration cleanup added or moved declarations across many melee, sysdolphin, MSL, MetroTRK, Runtime, and dolphin headers/sources, while also renaming many assembly call targets from anonymous func_80368xxx CObj accessors to HSD_CObjGetInterest, HSD_CObjGetEyePosition, and HSD_CObjGetUpVector. The PR body notes that after this pass, only sysdolphin remained. Review discussion centered on an mpcoll.c preprocessor workaround, where an #else was changed to #elif false because the alternate C implementation had a bad signature; reviewers accepted leaving it until someone matches/fixes it.
what_changed: Cleaned up implicit function declaration warnings across 47 files, with 416 added lines and 269 deleted lines in the PR slice.

Added or corrected prototypes in many headers, including sislib.h, gmmain_lib.h, mplib.h, devcom.h, grdynamicattr.h, lbunknown_004.h, jobj.h, lbfile.h, lbheap.h, lbdvd.h, pl_unknown_001.h, robj.h, dolphin/mtx.h, MetroTRK/intrinsics.h, and ft_unknown_006.h.

Moved a large block of SIS library declarations out of src/sysdolphin/baselib/sislib.c into src/sysdolphin/baselib/sislib.h, making those declarations reusable by other translation units instead of local-only.

Renamed assembly call targets for known HSD camera object helpers: func_803686AC became HSD_CObjGetInterest, func_80368784 became HSD_CObjGetEyePosition, and func_80368E70 became HSD_CObjGetUpVector in several asm files.

Touched assembly slices under camera, db_unknown_001, game-mode code_801601C4, grhomerun, grizumi, text_3, text_4, and sysdolphin/baselib/cobj.s to use named symbols instead of anonymous func_* labels where the target was identified.
Adjusted src/melee/mp/mpcoll.c around func_80043C6C by changing a preprocessor #else to #elif false, suppressing a bad-signature alternate branch rather than compiling it.
Removed or reduced declarations from some menu headers, including mncharsel.h and mntext.h, suggesting declarations were relocated or no longer needed there.
Made small build/config cleanup in obj_files.mk and removed one line from src/Runtime/platform.h; exact semantic reason is not visible in the excerpt.
smart_moves: Used proper public headers to resolve implicit declarations instead of relying on C89-style undeclared calls; this improves type checking and reduces future mismatch risk.
Converted known function addresses in assembly to semantic HSD_CObj* names, so remaining decomp work can search by behavior rather than opaque func_80368xxx labels.
Grouped SIS declarations into sislib.h, which is a reusable interface boundary and avoids duplicating or hiding declarations in a .c file.

Preserved known-bad matching work in mpcoll.c behind #elif false instead of forcing an incorrect signature into the build; the review explicitly notes the bad signature and acceptance to leave it for now.
Kept the PR focused on warning cleanup and symbol/prototype hygiene rather than trying to match every newly exposed problematic function immediately.
decomp_lessons: When eliminating implicit-function-declaration warnings, prefer adding the declaration to the owning subsystem header rather than locally forward-declaring it in arbitrary users.
Assembly symbol renames are useful even before full C decompilation: replacing func_* call targets with known library names improves grepability and validates prototypes across the project.

If a C fallback branch does not compile because the signature is wrong, disabling only that branch with a targeted preprocessor guard can be acceptable as a temporary matching tactic, but it should be documented by review or follow-up.

Moving declarations from .c files to .h files can be a sign that the project has discovered the real module interface; future PRs should reuse that interface instead of creating duplicate local prototypes.
Warning-cleanup passes can uncover bad signatures. Do not paper over these by inventing types; either match/fix the function or isolate the broken path clearly.
naming_conventions: Known HAL/SysDolphin camera object functions use HSD_CObj* Pascal-style subsystem prefixes, e.g. HSD_CObjGetInterest, HSD_CObjGetEyePosition, HSD_CObjGetUpVector.
Unknown decomp targets remain as func_<address> or lbl_<address> until identified.
Melee subsystem files and headers retain short lowercase prefixes such as lb, gm, gr, mp, mn, pl, and ft.
Header names generally mirror subsystem/source names, e.g. sislib.h for sislib.c, mplib.h for mp library declarations, and grdynamicattr.h for ground dynamic attribute declarations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renamed branch-and-link targets in assembly without changing instruction addresses or encoded branch destinations, preserving matching while improving symbol names.
Mapped func_803686AC to HSD_CObjGetInterest in many asm call sites including camera.s, db_unknown_001.s, code_801601C4.s, grhomerun.s, grizumi.s, text_3.s, and text_4.s.
Mapped func_80368784 to HSD_CObjGetEyePosition across camera and other asm slices.
Mapped func_80368E70 to HSD_CObjGetUpVector in db_unknown_001.s and text_4.s.
Used #elif false in mpcoll.c to prevent compilation of a branch with a bad function signature while keeping the code present for future matching work.

review_feedback.feedback: Questioned the change from #else to #elif false around func_80043C6C with the comment: "huh".
Explained that the disabled branch had a bad signature in the function and invited matching it instead.
Accepted the temporary workaround: "Ah okay we can leave it for now".

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that match or fix func_80043C6C in src/melee/mp/mpcoll.c and remove the #elif false workaround.
Search for remaining implicit-function-declaration cleanup passes after PR #682, especially in sysdolphin.
Search for further HSD_CObj function renames around func_8036885C, func_80368718, func_803687F0, func_803692E8, and func_80369BC8.
Check whether sislib.h became the canonical location for SIS library prototypes after this PR.
Look for later cleanup removing duplicate menu declarations from mncharsel.h or mntext.h.
